div.p: HTTP server reads shared state without blocking TCP message path

ol.li: Atomic Counters: publish_count, consume_count, error_count use sync/atomic — zero lock contention
RWMutex for State: brokerState uses sync.RWMutex — multiple readers (HTTP) OR single writer (TCP)
Read-Heavy Pattern: HTTP handlers acquire RLock (non-exclusive), TCP path uses RLock for reads, WLock only on topology changes
No Cross-Path Blocking: HTTP reads never block TCP writes because atomic ops + RWMutex readers don't block each other
